sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I see that G has 3 separate objects now:
- G at (59, 25), 1 px
- G at (59, 29), 1 px  
- G at (60, 27), 4 px
Total G pixels = 6
When I clicked M, G went from 1 to 6 pixels. This suggests clicking M when it's stuck actually makes G grow/regenerate.
Let me reconsider. The G object seems to split into multiple pieces when the blob gets close. Maybe the goal is to click each G piece individually to eliminate them. Let me try clicking all the G pixels.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# G split into 3 pieces: (59,25), (59,29), (60,27)
# Let me click each G pixel to eliminate them

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
g_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G']

print(f"G nodes: {len(g_nodes)}")
for g in g_nodes:
    print(f"  G: px={g['pixels']}, b={g['boundary']}")

# Click all G pixels
all_g_pixels = []
for g in g_nodes:
    all_g_pixels.extend(g['boundary'])

print(f"\nClicking {len(all_g_pixels)} G pixels...")
for p in all_g_pixels:
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': p[0], 'col': p[1]}])
    
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    nodes = seg['nodes']
    g_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G']
    g_px = sum(n['pixels'] for n in g_nodes) if g_nodes else 0
    
    print(f"After {p}: G_px={g_px}")
    if g_px == 0:
        print("G eliminated!")
        break
    if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
        print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
        break

# If G still exists, check state
if g_nodes:
    print(f"\nG still has {sum(n['pixels'] for n in g_nodes)} px")
    # Maybe I need to click the blob again to get it closer
    m_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'M']
    if m_nodes:
        m_pos = m_nodes[0]['boundary'][0]
        print(f"M at {m_pos}")
        # Try clicking adjacent to G
        for g in g_nodes:
            for gp in g['boundary']:
                action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': gp[0], 'col': gp[1]}])
                seg = current_frame.segmentation
                nodes = seg['nodes']
                g_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G']
                g_px = sum(n['pixels'] for n in g_nodes) if g_nodes else 0
                print(f"After G click {gp}: G_px={g_px}")
                if g_px == 0:
                    break
                if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
                    print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
                    break
            if not g_nodes:
                break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
